title: The RecruitNet database
text: RecruitNet dataset containing information plant-plant recruitment interactions 143 local plant communities (Study sites) 23 countries across five continents, including temperate tropical ecosystems. network provides information number individuals every “recruit” species recruiting proximity every “canopy” species away plants (called “open” interspaces). data set includes 850000 recruiting individuals involved 87924 different canopy-recruit pairs among 3186 vascular plant species. cover canopy species open ground also provided. published data paper Verdu et al. (2023)1 can downloaded : https://esajournals.onlinelibrary.wiley.com/doi/full/10.1002/ecy.3923 1Verdú, M., Garrido, J. L., Alcantara, J. M., Montesinos‐Navarro, ., Aguilar, S., Aizen, M. ., … & Zamora, R. (2023). RecruitNet: global database plant recruitment networks. Ecology 104(2):e3923. objectives script : (1) extracting RecruitNet database information one study sites, (2) define optional data structures required different analyses. starting, always good idea familiarize contentes dataset reading Metadata file provided paper. RecruitNet contains two “csv” files: RecruitNet.csv, bulk data canopy-recruit interactions, CanopyCover.csv data cover species study site.

text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. cover_data data frame abundance canopy species plot. int_type Indicates type plant-plant interaction analyzed: general recruitment, recruitment enhancement (.e. facilitation) recruitment depression (.e. competition). Explanation options: rec: pairwise interactions observed output. Focuses canopy-recruit interactions considering every recruit growing canopy another plant may occupy space future, thus potentially positive effect recruit species population. Therefore, even single observation considered interaction. type networks considers every species present study system, whether canopy recruit, node network. also includes "Open" particular node since species may recruit away established plants. Non-detected interactions also considered since zero frequency can provide evidence negative interaction expected frequency canopy species large. fac: pairwise interactions resulted recruitment enhancement output. Focuses interactions significantly higher recruitment density canopy  "Open" (.e. facilitation).  Non-detected interactions considered "Open" included node, although relative cover considered part sampling area. comp: pairwise interactions resulted recruitment depression output. Focuses interactions significantly lower recruitment density canopy "Open" (.e. competition). Non-detected interactions considered (.e. expanding 0 possible interactions study system), absence recruitment species given canopy can reflect particularly strong depression recruitment canopy species. "Open" included node, although relative cover considered part sampling area.

title: Basic network dimensions — RN_dims
text: table following information: Num nodes: Number nodes network (N). case facilitation competition nodes provided guild Num. links: Number links network (L). Open represented row zeroes adjacency matrix). facilitation recruitment depressing networks, connectance calculated $C = L /(N_c N_r)$, N_c N_r number canopy recruit species, respectively. int_type**: Indicates type plant-plant interaction presented output (recruitment patterns, recruitment enhancement (.e. facilitation) recruitment depression (.e. competition)) rec: Estimates number nodes, links connectance network based plant-plant interactions contribute recruitment. "Open" considered additional canopy species category. fac: Estimates number nodes, links connectance network based pairwise interactions significantly enhance  recruitment. every interaction detected field present matrix. Non-detected interactions considered "Open" included canopy species category. comp: Estimates number nodes, links connectance network based pairwise interactions depress recruitment. every interaction detected field present matrix. title: Visualize a heatmap of the interactions matrix. — RN_heatmap
text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. cover_data data frame abundance canopy species plot. int_type #' Indicates type plant-plant interaction presented output matrix: general recruitment, recruitment enhancement (.e. facilitation) recruitment depression (.e. competition). may take three possible values: rec: Estimates node degree based plant-plant interaction contribute recruitment. fac: Estimates node degree based pairwise interactions enhance recruitment. every observed interaction field present matrix. case non-detected interactions considered "Open" included canopy species category. comp: Estimates node degree based pairwise interactions depress recruitment. every observed interaction field present matrix. However, case non-detected interactions considered (.e. expanding 0 possible interactions study system). "Open" included canopy species category. weight Specifies metric used represent interaction strength (.e., weight) assigned pair species matrix. See function associndex details. possible options : Fcr: frequency recruitment number recruits canopy-recruit pair. Dcr: density recruitment number recruits per unit area canopy species. Ns: index Normalized Neighbour Suitability index (proposed Mingo, 2014), suitable comparisons interaction strength pairs species within local community, preferred general recruitment networks (Alcantara et al. 2025). NIntA: index additive symmetry intensity index proposed Diaz-Sierra et al. (2017). NIntC: index commutative symmetry intensity index proposed Diaz-Sierra et al. (2017). RII: index Relative Interaction Index (Armas et al., 2004).

what: Convert field data to matrix format — RN_to_matrix
title: Convert field data to matrix format — RN_to_matrix
text: converts community data collected field, row represents single canopy-recruit interaction, matrix format, recruit species rows canopy species columns. general recruitment networks, matrix square including species observed study site. means set species appears rows columns, "Open" areas treated additional category Canopy Recruit variables. contrast, recruitment enhancement (.e. facilitation) depression (.e. competition), matrix can non-square. case, rows represent species whose recruitment enhanced suppressed, columns represent canopy species influence recruitment. two groups may include species.

title: Convert field data to matrix format — RN_to_matrix
code: RN_to_matrix(   int_data,   cover_data,   int_type = c("rec", "fac", "comp"),   weight = c("Fcr", "Dcr", "Dro", "Ns", "NintC", "NintA", "RII") )

title: Convert field data to matrix format — RN_to_matrix
text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. cover_data data frame abundance canopy species plot. int_type Indicates type plant-plant interaction analyzed: general recruitment, recruitment enhancement (.e. facilitation) recruitment depression (.e. competition). Explanation options: rec: pairwise interactions observed output. Focuses canopy-recruit interactions considering every recruit growing canopy another plant may occupy space future, thus potentially positive effect recruit species population. Therefore, even single observation considered interaction. type networks considers every species present study system, whether canopy recruit, node network. also includes "Open" particular node since species may recruit away established plants. Non-detected interactions also considered since zero frequency can provide evidence negative interaction expected frequency canopy species large. fac: pairwise interactions resulted recruitment enhancement output. Focuses interactions significantly higher recruitment density canopy  "Open" (.e. facilitation).  Non-detected interactions considered "Open" included node, although relative cover considered part sampling area. comp: pairwise interactions resulted recruitment depression output. Focuses interactions significantly lower recruitment density canopy "Open" (.e. competition). Non-detected interactions considered (.e. expanding 0 possible interactions study system), absence recruitment species given canopy can reflect particularly strong depression recruitment canopy species. "Open" included node, although relative cover considered part sampling area. weight Specifies metric used represent interaction strength (.e., weight) assigned pair species matrix. Explanation options (mathematical information description function associndex() : Fcr: frequency recruitment, number recruits canopy-recruit pair. Dcr: density recruitment, number recruits per unit area canopy species cover. Dro: density recruitment open interspaces, number recruits per unit area open interspaces. Ns: Normalized Neighbour Suitability index; suitable comparisons interaction strength pairs species within local community. NIntA: Additive symmetry intensity index. NIntC: Commutative symmetry intensity index. RII: Relative Interaction Index.

title: Convert field data to matrix format — RN_to_matrix
text: matrix recruit species rows canopy species columns, cells describing measure selected describe interaction strength (.e. weight) pair species.

what: Accumulation curve of network dimensions — accum_curve
title: Accumulation curve of network dimensions — accum_curve
text: Uses plot accumulation curves assess whether estimates number nodes, links, link density (.k.. connectance) stable (see Pulgar et al. 2017 descriptors). function accum_curve plots accumulation curves parameters can obtained package igraph (Csardi & Nepusz, 2006). NOTE: function intended data sets organised multiple plots community locality.

title: Accumulation curve of network dimensions — accum_curve
Accumulation curve of network dimensions — accum_curve
text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. property property: indicates network property, obtained igraph functions, accuracy evaluated. three options currently available: vcount: accuracy estimated number nodes (using igraph function "vcount"). ecount: accuracy estimated number canopy-recruit interactions (using igraph function "ecount"). edge_density: accuracy estimated network connectance (using igraph function "edge_density). k integer number specifying number random repetitions subsets n plots. k repetitions, subset n randomly chosen plots combined build partial network indicated property estimated. High values provide confident estimates accuracy, function may take long time k >> 100.

title: Accumulation curve of network dimensions — accum_curve
text: function returns list two objects: plot representing mean 95% Confidence interval (.e. 1.96 times standard error) estimate property selected increasing number randomly selected plots considered. data frame cumulative values property repetition k plots. Provided can prepare customized accumulation plot.

title: Accumulation curve of network dimensions — accum_curve
code: accum_links <- accum_curve(Amoladeras_int, property="ecount", k=10) head(accum_links$Data) #>   Value sampleSize #> 1    53          1 #> 2    49          1 #> 3    49          1 #> 4    55          1 #> 5    55          1 #> 6    43          1 accum_links$Plot

what: Calculate different association indices considering different interaction types. — associndex
title: Calculate different association indices considering different interaction types. — associndex
text: Calculates different metrics characterize interaction strength pairwise interaction, based number recruits found canopy species "Open" ground. Several indices proposed assess much recruitment affected canopy species relative species performance absence canopy plants (.e. open), particularities. context recruitment interactions, frequently used index Relative Interaction Index (RII; Armas et al., 2004), studies recently used additive commutative symmetry intensity indices (NIntA NIntC) y Diaz-Sierra et al. (2017). However, indices well suited comparisons interaction strength pairs species within local community, Normalized Neighbour Suitability index (Ns; Mingo, 2014) preferred (Alcantara et al. 2025). function associndex calculates four indices besides providing density recruits canopy open, data used calculate (relative cover number recruits microhabitat). **IMPORTANT NOTE: Data recruitment Open required calculate indices. Input: canopy-recruit interactions data set canopy cover data set

title: Calculate different association indices considering different interaction types. — associndex
code: associndex(   int_data,   cover_data,   expand = c("yes", "no"),   rm_sp_no_cover = c("allsp", "onlycanopy"),   threshold_density = NULL )

title: Calculate different association indices considering different interaction types. — associndex
text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. cover_data data frame abundance canopy species plot. expand Expands data frame include non-detected interactions every pair species occur study site, assigning values 0 variables Fcr Dcr. Explanation options: yes: Generates possible interactions canopy recruit species study site (.e. detected non-detected), adding values interaction frequency 0 non-detected interactions. : Considers pairwise interactions actually detected study site. rm_sp_no_cover rm_sp_no_cover: Defines filtering behavior, specifying species removed based availability cover data type interaction interest. Explanation options: allsp: removes species lacks cover data (canopy recruit species). onlycanopy: removes canopy species lacking cover data, retaining recruit species even lack cover data. threshold_density threshold_density: indicates threshold retain interactions observed density threshold (.e. Dcr < thr & Dro < thr). required avoid use interactions based recruits may occurred chance scarce canopy species. cases result large overestimation Dcr, likely spurious outlayers. threshold value provided set NULL, function estimates threshold value based recruitment density outliers according Weibull distribution: value outlier limit less 1 observation expected. Explanation options: positive value. High values provide chance include potentially spurious interactions, unless high density reliable based knowledge study system. way decide threshold value consist identifying possible outlayers distribution recruitment densities (Dcr Dro). NULL: use package extremevalues find density threshold value (K) , according Weibull distribution, less 1 observation expected given number observations sample.

title: Calculate different association indices considering different interaction types. — associndex
text: data frame following information canopy-recruit interaction following information: Canopy: canopy species. Recruit: recruit species. Fcr: frequency recruitment, number recruits found canopy species. Ac: Area occupied canopy species study site, m^2^ (m relative cover measured transects). Fro: frequency recruitment, number recruits Open interspaces. - Ao: Area  occupied open interspaces study site m^2^ (m relative cover measured transects). Dcr: Density recruits canopy species: $Fcr/Ac$. Dro: Density recruits open interspaces: $Fro/Ao$. max_Recr: maximum recruitment density species study site (.e., canopy species "Open"). Ns: Normalized Neighbour Suitability index: $(Dcr - Dro) / maxRecr$. NintC: Commutative symmetry intensity index: $2*(Dcr - Dro)/((Dcr + Dro)+abs(Dcr-Dro))$ NintA: Additive symmetry intensity index: $2*(Dcr - Dro)/((Dro) + abs(Dcr-Dro))$ RII: Relative Interaction Index: $(Dcr - Dro)/(Dcr + Dro)$

title: Check cover data format — check_cover
text: canopy cover data set contains abundance canopy species plot. minimum columns required : Plot. interactions data set. Canopy. interactions data set. Cover. Cover canopy species ("Open" interspaces), measured % total area sampled recruit ascribed interaction canopy plant (recruiting "Open", away established plants). example, following Alcantara et al. (2019), plants branches less 1.5m ground (e.g. small shrubs treelets), area projection canopy ground, tall trees can area extending 0.5m around trunk's base large surfacing roots. Sampled_distance_or_area. Total area plot distance transect (m^2^ m, respectively).

text: canopy-recruit interactions data set must contain, least, raw data observed frequency canopy-recruit pair sampling unit (e.g. plot, quadrat, transect). standardization, columns data set must named: Plot. Plot ID. Uniquely identifies sampling units interactions surveyed. Include column even surveyed one plot. Canopy. Name canopy species. survey included type open interspaces (e.g., canopy gaps, open ground, spaces away canopy plant), include single node named "Open" (note capital "O"). use full scientific names (e.g. latin binomials), concatenate epithets lower dash (e.g. Olea_europaea). Recruit. Name recruit species. use full scientific names, concatenate epithets lower dash (e.g. Olea_europaea). Frequency. Frequency canopy-recruit interaction study plot, indicated number individuals recruit species found individuals canopy species.

what: Combine cover data with interaction data. — comm_to_RN
title: Combine cover data with interaction data. — comm_to_RN
text: Combine interactions cover data sets study site single data frame. study sites data collected several plots, function collapses information single value per pairwise interaction. can choose whether output contains possible pairs interacting species (including observed, assigned weight = 0) whether remove species lacking information canopy cover.

title: Combine cover data with interaction data. — comm_to_RN
code: comm_to_RN(   int_data,   cover_data,   expand = c("yes", "no"),   rm_sp_no_cover = c("allsp", "onlycanopy") )

title: Combine cover data with interaction data. — comm_to_RN
text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. cover_data data frame abundance canopy species plot. expand Indicates whether expand dataframe include non-detected interactions every pair species occurs study site, assigning values 0 frequency. can take two possible values: yes: Generates possible interactions canopy recruit species study site (.e. detected non-detected), adding values interaction strength 0 non-detected interactions. : Considers pairwise interactions actually detected study site. rm_sp_no_cover Defines filtering behavior, specifying species removed based availability cover data. can take two options: allsp: removes species lacking cover data (canopy recruit species). onlycanopy: removes canopy species lacking cover data, retaining recruit species even lack cover data.

title: Combine cover data with interaction data. — comm_to_RN
text: Data frame following information pair species: Canopy. Name canopy species, including "Open" interspaces. use full scientific names, concatenate epithets lower dash (e.g. Olea_europaea). Recruit. Name recruit species. use full scientific names, concatenate epithets lower dash (e.g. Olea_europaea). Ac: Area (m^2^), (m relative cover measured transects) occupied canopy species ("Open" interspaces) total area sampled study site. Ar. Area (m^2^), (m relative cover measured transects) occupied recruit species (observed adults), total area sampled study site. Fcr: frequency recruitment, number recruits canopy-recruit pair. Icr: incidence interaction, number plots observed. Pcr: presence/absence (1/0) interaction study site. provides "unweighted" version adjacency matrix.

title: Combine cover data with interaction data. — comm_to_RN
code: df <- comm_to_RN(Amoladeras_int, Amoladeras_cover, expand="yes", rm_sp_no_cover="allsp") head(df) #>                  Canopy              Recruit Fcr Icr Pcr Ac      Ar #> 1  Artemisia_barrelieri Artemisia_barrelieri   3   1   1  1  1.0000 #> 22 Artemisia_barrelieri Artemisia_campestris   0   0   0  1  0.1100 #> 20 Artemisia_barrelieri      Asparagus_albus   0   0   0  1 27.7250 #> 16 Artemisia_barrelieri   Asparagus_horridus   0   0   0  1  1.5725 #> 12 Artemisia_barrelieri      Ballota_hirsuta   0   0   0  1 38.4550 #> 13 Artemisia_barrelieri Helichrysum_stoechas   3   1   1  1 29.0700

what: Species functional role in the general recruitment network — funtopol_rec
title: Species functional role in the general recruitment network — funtopol_rec
text: provides summary information frequency species functional role classification species role.

title: Species functional role in the general recruitment network — funtopol_rec
Species functional role in the general recruitment network — funtopol_rec
text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. cover_data data frame abundance canopy species plot.

title: Species functional role in the general recruitment network — funtopol_rec
text: list two elements, one descriptors networks another role species. first element list provides following information: Num. nodes: Total number nodes network. Num. edges: Total number links network. Connectance:  Proportion links observed relative possible links (C). case recruitment networks, use formula $C = L /(N^2 - N)$ since node "Open" act recruit (.e. Open represented row zeroes adjacency matrix). Num. non-trivial SCCs: Number SCCs formed one node network. Num. core species: Number species core SCC. Prop. core species: Proportion species core relative total number species network. Num. satellite species: Number satellite species network. Prop. satellite species: Proportion satellite species relative total number species network. Num. disturbance-dependent transients: Number disturbance-dependent transient species network. Prop. disturbance-dependent transients: Proportion disturbance-dependent transient species relative total number species network. Num. strict transients: Number strict transient species network. Prop. strict transients: Proportion strict transient species relative total number species network. Qualitative Persistence: sum proportion core satellite species. second element list, provides role plant species network, whose interpretations differ plant-plant interaction type. node (.e. species) can : Part core core. case general recruitment networks, direction links represents space occupied canopy species (predecessor node) replaced species recruiting canopy (successor node). case, species core must recruit, least, canopy another core species, allow recruitment least another core species. satellite species: non-core species can reached core species, following direction arrows. general recruitment networks, species recruit canopy core species show recruitment core species canopy. disturbance-dependent transient species: species can reached "Open" node core satellite species (.e. example, pioneer species recruit away established plants). strict transient species: species reached node. case general recruitment networks strict transients species recruit studied local assemblage (e.g., pioneer species mature forest patch relict species distribution limit).

title: Statistical significance of interactions — int_significance
text: conducts statistical tests pairwise interaction, indicating whether effect canopy species recruitment enhancing (.e. positive), depressing (.e. negative), neutral, whether tested due low sample size. IMPORTANT NOTE: Data recruitment Open required tests. assess whether recruitment affected given canopy species compared "Open" used exact binomial test chi square test (number recruits large enough expected frequencies larger 5). tests address null hypothesis recruitment frequent given canopy species open interspaces. Thus, use tests goodness--fit tests. logic , recruitment neutral regarding microhabitat, observe number recruits canopy exactly proportional relative cover microhabitat. null hypothesis rejected, conclude recruitment affected (enhanced depressed) canopy species compared prospects recruitment seeds dispersed away established plants. exact binomial test applied canopy-recruit pairs low number recruits, may impossible reject null hypothesis even recruits occurred less likely microhabitat. cases, one might conclude interaction neutral effect actually possible reach conclusion. classify cases "testable".

title: Statistical significance of interactions — int_significance
Statistical significance of interactions — int_significance
text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. cover_data data frame abundance canopy species plot. int_type Indicates type plant-plant interaction analyzed: general recruitment, recruitment enhancement (.e. facilitation) recruitment depression (.e. competition). Explanation options: rec: pairwise interactions observed output. Focuses canopy-recruit interactions considering every recruit growing canopy another plant may occupy space future, thus potentially positive effect recruit species population. Therefore, even single observation considered interaction. type networks considers every species present study system, whether canopy recruit, node network. also includes "Open" particular node since species may recruit away established plants. Non-detected interactions also considered since zero frequency can provide evidence negative interaction expected frequency canopy species large. fac: pairwise interactions resulted recruitment enhancement output. Focuses interactions significantly higher recruitment density canopy  "Open" (.e. facilitation).  Non-detected interactions considered "Open" included node, although relative cover considered part sampling area. comp: pairwise interactions resulted recruitment depression output. Focuses interactions significantly lower recruitment density canopy "Open" (.e. competition). Non-detected interactions considered (.e. expanding 0 possible interactions study system), absence recruitment species given canopy can reflect particularly strong depression recruitment canopy species. "Open" included node, although relative cover considered part sampling area.

title: Statistical significance of interactions — int_significance
text: data frame following information recruit-canopy interaction following information: Canopy: Canopy species Recruit: Recruit species Fcr: frequency recruitment, number recruits found canopy species. Ac: Area (m^2^), (m relative cover measured transects) occupied canopy species study site. Fro: frequency recruitment open interspaces. Ao: Area (m^2^), (m relative cover measured transects) occupied open interspaces study site. testability: Testability indicates smallest p-value binomial test estimate based given number recruits. p-value reference p-value (typically 0.05), cases (number recruits) ever reject null hypothesis, interaction testable. Significance: p-value chi square binomial test assessing null hypothesis Fcr Fro equal expected frequencies based relative cover two microhabitats: $Ac/(Ac+Ao)$ $1-(Ac/(Ac+Ao))$ Test_type: Indicates whether, depending sample size, chi square binomial test conducted. Effect_int: Indicates, interaction, whether analysis classifies "Enhancing", "Depressing", "Neutral" "Testable".

title: Statistical significance of interactions — int_significance
code: int_signif_rec <- int_significance(Amoladeras_int, Amoladeras_cover, int_type = "rec") #> Different tests were used for different canopy-recruit pairs. #>               Check column Test_type what: Estimates the completeness and coverage of the sampled interactions — link_completeness
title: Estimates the completeness and coverage of the sampled interactions — link_completeness
text: Estimates completeness coverage sampled interactions, providing probability detecting new canopy-recruit pair increased sampling effort one unit (.e., locating one recruit surveying one plot). function uses iNEXT::iNEXT() (Hsieh et al. 2016).

title: Estimates the completeness and coverage of the sampled interactions — link_completeness
Estimates the completeness and coverage of the sampled interactions — link_completeness
text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. type indicates whether completeness coverage calculated based number individual recruits canopy species (.e., abundance) presence/absence canopy-recruit pairs across plots (.e., incidence). survey based multiple independent plots, good practice use "incidence" approach. "abundance" approach can used generally, assumes recruit represents independent canopy-recruit interaction event, may easily overestimate coverage recruits species tend occur aggregated canopy plant (occurs frequently). Explanation options: incidence: Uses incidence given interaction number plots observed. abundance: Uses abundance given interaction number recruits canopy-recruit.

title: Estimates the completeness and coverage of the sampled interactions — link_completeness
text: data frame indicating number links observed (Lobs), number links estimated (Lest), completeness sampling links proportion canopy-recruit pairs observed expected study site (Completeness links: Lobs/Lest) probability one recruit (plot) sampled, corresponded (contained) already detected canopy-recruit species interaction (Coverage links).

title: Estimates the completeness and coverage of the sampled interactions — link_completeness
code: link_completeness(Amoladeras_int, type="abundance") #> Warning: Abundance-based approach assumes that each individual recruit provides independent data about the canopy-recruit interaction. If conspecific recruits frequently occur aggregated under individual canopy plants, the estimates of completeness and coverage may be severely overestimated. #> Warning: Your data is structured in multiple plots. title: Calculate width of canopy service and recruitment niches — node_degrees
Calculate width of canopy service and recruitment niches — node_degrees
text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. cover_data data frame abundance canopy species plot. int_type Indicates type plant-plant interaction presented output matrix: general recruitment, recruitment enhancement (.e. facilitation) recruitment depression (.e. competition). may take three possible values: rec: Estimates node degree based plant-plant interaction contribute recruitment. fac: Estimates node degree based pairwise interactions enhance recruitment. every observed interaction field present matrix. case non-detected interactions considered "Open" included canopy species category. comp: Estimates node degree based pairwise interactions depress recruitment. every observed interaction field present matrix. However, case non-detected interactions considered (.e. expanding 0 possible interactions study system). "Open" included canopy species category.

title: Calculate width of canopy service and recruitment niches — node_degrees
text: output depends type interaction considered. recruitment networks returns single data frame following information: Node: plant species (either canopy recruit) Ac: Area canopy species ("Open" interspaces), measured distance (m, transects) area (m^2^, plots) used estimate canopy cover study site. canopy_service_width: number species recruit given canopy species (.e., canopy species' -degree); can interpreted width canopy service. canopy_contribution: number recruits species associated canopy species. degrees weighted frequency recruitment (Fcr), can interpreted contribution canopy species multispecific sapling bank. effective_canopy_service: weighted degrees transformed effective number partners, accounting dominance certain interactions recruit bank. effective canopy service estimated e^H, H Shannon's index diversity calculated canopy species Fcr. recruitment_niche_width: number canopy species allow recruitment (recruit species' -degree); can interpreted width recruitment niche. recruit_bank_abundance: number recruits species study site. degrees weighted frequency recruitment (Fcr), can interpreted species abundance recruit bank. effective_recruitment_niche: weighted degrees transformed effective number partners, accounting dominance certain interactions recruit bank. effective recruitment niche width estimated $e^H$, H Shannon's index diversity calculated recruit species Fcr. facilitation recruitment depressing interactions, output consists two data frames, one  canopy species another recruit species. data frame information canopy species provides following information: Nurse (Canopy) sp: Canopy species. Ac: Area canopy species ("Open" interspaces), measured distance (m, transects) area (m^2^, plots) used estimate canopy cover study site. N enhanced (depressed) recruit sp: number species whose recruitment enhanced (depressed) canopy, can interpreted width enhancing (depressing) effect. data frame information recruit species contains following information: Facilitated (recruit) sp: Recruit species. N_ind: number recruits facilitated (depressed) species study site. N enhancing (depressing) canopy sp: number canopy species enhance recruitment (.e. nurse species) depress (.e. competing species). can interpreted width niche nurses competitors.

title: Summary and node-based information of species in loops and simple paths — topol_depre
text: list two elements, one information intransitive loops recruitment depression another information simple indirect recruitment depression linear paths beginning ending species. first element list loops provides following information within two levels: summary: data frame many rows SCC present recruitment depression network (one species, autodepression considered) columns, scc identifier scc_id number species involved SCC n_nodos nodes: list many elements SCCs found, including vector name species involved SCC second element list simple, provides following information within two levels: summary: data frame many rows distinct simple linear indirect recruitment depression paths (.e. overlap species composition less 75%) begin ("") end ("") species, including linear versions SCCs. columns, species distinct linear path begin end  nodo, numeric index identifies paths within species path_index number species involved path n_nodes_in_path includes focal species, independently whether autodepression. nodes: list many elements species recruitment depression network (either depresor depressed), including many elements distinct paths begin ends , , vector name species involved simple indirect recruitment depression linear paths. arguments (options)**: direction = c("","") Argument 1. direction: Indicates direction indirect recruitment depression assessed. Explanation options: : Estimates paths based incoming links node, representing set canopy species affect recruitment given species depressive effects, either directly (depressing recruitment) indirectly (depressing recruitment canopy species connected recruitment depression interactions). Link direction considered reciprocal simple paths. : Estimates paths based outgoing links nodes, representing set species canopy species affect depressive effects, either directly indirectly (.e. intermediate species). direction links applied reciprocal simple paths

text: Plant-plant interaction network visualization graph. one hand, visualization nodes interactions, can visualized two formats, either graph conducted function adjacency matrix, function RN_heatmap. hand, three functions, one interaction type network, visualize functional topology general recruitment networks visu_funtopol_rec structural topology recruitment enhancement visu_topol_fac depression visu_topol_depre networks respectively.

title: Graph of nodes and interactions — visu_net
code: visu_net(   int_data,   cover_data,   int_type = c("rec", "fac", "comp"),   weight = c("Pcr", "Fcr", "Dcr", "Dro", "Ns", "NintC", "NintA", "RII"),   mode = c("uni", "bi"),   scale_w = 1 )

title: Graph of nodes and interactions — visu_net
text: int_data data frame containing interaction data. cover_data data frame abundance canopy species plot. int_type Indicates type plant-plant interaction analyzed: general recruitment, recruitment enhancement (.e. facilitation) recruitment depression (.e. competition). See detailed options int_significance(). weight specifies metric used represent interaction strength (.e., weight) assigned pair species matrix. Explanation options (mathematical information description function associndex): Fcr: frequency recruitment number recruits canopy-recruit pair. Dcr: density recruitment number recruits per unit area canopy species. Ns: index Normalized Neighbour Suitability index (proposed Mingo, 2014), suitable comparisons interaction strength pairs species within local community, preferred general recruitment networks (Alcantara et al. 2025). NIntA: index additive symmetry intensity index proposed Diaz-Sierra et al. (2017). NIntC: index commutative symmetry intensity index proposed Diaz-Sierra et al. (2017). -RII: index Relative Interaction Index (Armas et al., 2004). mode used recruitment enhancement("fac") recruitment depression ("comp") networks. Indicates whether network plotted unipartite bipartite network. bipartite networks, canopy species shown upper row recruits lower row graph. general recruitment networks, network considered unipartite, result error argument given option "bi". scale_w argument proportionally increase decrease thickness links. networks, high values can result overlapping links difficult visualization.

title: Graph of nodes and interactions — visu_net
text: graph representing network plant-plant interactions.

code: # Unipartite network representation of a general recruitment network. Link width # corresponds to the scaled frequency of recruitment (*Fcr*): visu_net(mysite_com, mysite_cov, int_type="rec", weight="Fcr", mode="uni", scale_w=0.01) #> Error: object 'mysite_com' not found  # Unipartite representation of a facilitation network. Link width corresponds to the # scaled *Ns* index: visu_net(mysite_com, mysite_cov, int_type="fac", weight="Ns", mode="uni", scale_w=5) #> Error: object 'mysite_com' not found  # Bipartite representation of a facilitation network. Link width corresponds to the # scaled *Ns* index. Canopy species are shown in the upper row and recruits in the lower # row of the graph: visu_net(mysite_com, mysite_cov, int_type="fac", weight="Ns", mode="bi", scale_w=5) #> Error: object 'mysite_com' not found  # Unipartite representation of a recruitment depression (*competition*) network. Link # width corresponds to the scaled *RII* index: visu_net(mysite_com, mysite_cov, int_type="comp", weight="RII", mode="uni", scale_w=5) #> Error: object 'mysite_com' not found  # Bipartite representation of a recruitment depression (*competition*) network. Link # width corresponds to the scaled *RII* index.
